prompt.fr: Mettez au passĂ© composĂ© : Â« Hier, jâ€™___ (terminer) le dossier. Â»

explanation.correct_why: avoir (ai) + participe passĂ© (terminĂ©) = Â« ai terminĂ© Â». No agreement under avoir without a preceding object.

distractor_why.infinitive: Â« ai terminer Â» uses the infinitive; need the participle terminĂ©.
distractor_why.agreement: Â« ai terminĂ©e Â» adds agreement, but no preceding direct object here.

explanation.grammar_rule: PassĂ© composĂ© = auxiliaire au prĂ©sent + participe passĂ©.

prompt.fr: Choisissez lâ€™auxiliaire : Â« Elle ___ partie Ă  midi. Â»

explanation.correct_why: Â« partir Â» is an ĂŞtre-verb â†’ Â« est Â», and the participle agrees: Â« est partie Â» (feminine).

distractor_why.avoir_for_etre: Â« a Â» is avoir; partir takes ĂŞtre.
distractor_why.wrong_person: Â« es Â» is the tu form.

explanation.grammar_rule: Movement verbs (partir, arriver, venirâ€¦) take ĂŞtre; participle agrees with the subject.

tip.pattern: ĂŞtre + participle â†’ agree with subject.

prompt.fr: Corrigez : Â« Nous avons allĂ©s Ă  la rĂ©union. Â»

accepted: Nous sommes allĂ©s Ă  la rĂ©union
Nous sommes allĂ©s Ă  la rĂ©union.

explanation.correct_why: Â« aller Â» is an ĂŞtre-verb â†’ Â« sommes Â», and the participle agrees with nous (plural): Â« allĂ©s Â».

distractor_why.kept_avoir: Â« avons allĂ©s Â» uses avoir; aller takes ĂŞtre.
distractor_why.no_agreement: Under ĂŞtre, the participle must agree (allĂ©s).

explanation.grammar_rule: aller â†’ ĂŞtre; participle agrees with the subject.
explanation.vocab_notes: aller Ă  la rĂ©union = to go to the meeting.

tip.memory_aid: aller, venir, arriver, partir â†’ ĂŞtre.

prompt.fr: Mettez au passĂ© composĂ© : Â« La gestionnaire ___ (recevoir) les commentaires. Â»

explanation.correct_why: recevoir is irregular: participle Â« reĂ§u Â»; with avoir â†’ Â« a reĂ§u Â».

distractor_why.regularized: Â« a recevu Â» regularizes an irregular participle; it is Â« reĂ§u Â».
distractor_why.agreement: No preceding direct object, so no agreement.

explanation.grammar_rule: Some participles are irregular: recevoirâ†’reĂ§u, Ă©crireâ†’Ă©crit, faireâ†’fait, prendreâ†’pris.
explanation.vocab_notes: recevoir â†’ reĂ§u; les commentaires = feedback.

prompt.fr: Associez lâ€™infinitif Ă  son participe passĂ©.
prompt.instructions_en: Match each verb to its past participle.

explanation.correct_why: faireâ†’fait, prendreâ†’pris, Ă©crireâ†’Ă©crit, recevoirâ†’reĂ§u, mettreâ†’mis.
explanation.grammar_rule: Many high-frequency verbs have irregular participles to memorize.
explanation.vocab_notes: These five appear constantly in workplace writing.
common_mistakes: regularizing (e.g. Â« prendu Â» instead of Â« pris Â»)

similar: Jâ€™ai mis Ă  jour le dossier.

prompt.fr: PassĂ© composĂ© : Â« Nous ___ (attendre) la dĂ©cision. Â»

explanation.correct_why: avoir (avons) + participle attendu (-reâ†’-u) = Â« avons attendu Â».

distractor_why.wrong_pp: -re verbs give -u participles: attendre â†’ attendu.
distractor_why.infinitive: Â« avons attendre Â» uses the infinitive.

explanation.grammar_rule: -re participle = -u (vendu, attendu, rĂ©pondu).

prompt.fr: Mettez Ă  lâ€™imparfait : Â« Avant, je ___ (travailler) Ă  MontrĂ©al. Â»

explanation.correct_why: Stem travaill- + -ais (je) = Â« travaillais Â».

distractor_why.present: Â« travaille Â» is the present.
distractor_why.passe_compose: Â« ai travaillĂ© Â» is the passĂ© composĂ©.

explanation.grammar_rule: Imparfait je â†’ radical + -ais.
common_mistakes: using present or passĂ© composĂ© for a past habit

tip.memory_aid: Habit/background in the past â†’ imparfait.

prompt.fr: Choisissez le temps : Â« Quand le tĂ©lĂ©phone a sonnĂ©, je ___ un courriel. Â»
prompt.instructions_en: Background action interrupted â†’ which form?

explanation.correct_why: The ongoing background action uses the imparfait: Â« jâ€™Ă©crivais Â» (was writing) when the phone rang (passĂ© composĂ©).

distractor_why.pc_for_background: Â« ai Ă©crit Â» would mean a completed action, not the ongoing background.
distractor_why.present: Â« Ă©cris Â» is present, not past.

explanation.grammar_rule: Imparfait = ongoing/background; passĂ© composĂ© = the interrupting completed event.
common_mistakes: using passĂ© composĂ© for the background action

similar: Je lisais quand il est arrivĂ©.
Elle parlait quand on a frappĂ©.

prompt.fr: Mettez Ă  lâ€™imparfait : Â« Nous ___ (ĂŞtre) en rĂ©union Ă  10 h. Â»

explanation.correct_why: ĂŞtre has the irregular imparfait stem Ă©t-: nous â†’ Â« Ă©tions Â».

distractor_why.wrong_stem: ĂŞtre does not use the regular nous-stem; it uses Ă©t-.

explanation.grammar_rule: ĂŞtre imparfait: jâ€™Ă©tais, tu Ă©tais, il Ă©tait, nous Ă©tions, vous Ă©tiez, ils Ă©taient.

tip.pattern: Only the stem is irregular; endings are normal.

prompt.fr: ComplĂ©tez : Â« Tous les lundis, elle ___ (prĂ©parer) lâ€™ordre du jour. Â»
prompt.instructions_en: Repeated past habit â†’ which form/ending?

explanation.correct_why: Â« Tous les lundis Â» signals a repeated past habit â†’ imparfait: Â« prĂ©parait Â».

distractor_why.pc_for_habit: PassĂ© composĂ© marks a single completed event, not a habit.
distractor_why.present: Present doesn't place it in the past.

explanation.grammar_rule: Repeated/habitual past actions use the imparfait.

tip.pattern: Habit markers (tous lesâ€¦, souvent) â†’ imparfait.
similar: Chaque jour, il classait les dossiers.

prompt.fr: Imparfait : Â« Nous ___ (avoir) plus de personnel Ă  lâ€™Ă©poque. Â»

explanation.correct_why: avoir nous-stem av- + -ions = Â« avions Â».

distractor_why.wrong: Â« avaisons Â» is not a form.

explanation.grammar_rule: Imparfait stem = nous-present minus -ons (avonsâ†’av-).

prompt.fr: Description ou Ă©vĂ©nement ? Â« Il ___ beau et tout le monde Ă©tait de bonne humeur. Â»

explanation.correct_why: Weather/setting in the past is description â†’ imparfait Â« faisait Â».

distractor_why.pc: PassĂ© composĂ© marks a completed event, not a description.

explanation.grammar_rule: Descriptions and settings in the past use the imparfait.
explanation.vocab_notes: il fait beau = the weather is nice.

similar: Il y avait beaucoup de monde.

prompt.fr: Futur simple : Â« La semaine prochaine, nous ___ (terminer) le projet. Â»

explanation.correct_why: Infinitive terminer + -ons (nous) = Â« terminerons Â».

distractor_why.conditional: Â« terminerions Â» is the conditional.

explanation.grammar_rule: Futur simple = infinitif + -ai/-as/-a/-ons/-ez/-ont.

prompt.fr: ComplĂ©tez : Â« Demain, je ___ (ĂŞtre) au bureau Ă  8 h. Â»

explanation.correct_why: ĂŞtre has the irregular future stem ser-: je â†’ Â« serai Â».
explanation.grammar_rule: ĂŞtre future: serai, seras, sera, serons, serez, seront.
common_mistakes: confusing future serai with conditional serais

tip.pattern: Memorize irregular future stems (ser-, aur-, ir-, fer-).

prompt.fr: Futur simple : Â« Quand le budget ___ (ĂŞtre) approuvĂ©, nous embaucherons. Â»
prompt.instructions_en: After 'quand' referring to the future, use the future.

explanation.correct_why: After Â« quand Â» referring to a future event, French uses the future: Â« quand le budget sera approuvĂ© Â».

distractor_why.present_after_quand: Unlike English ('when it is'), French uses the future after quand for future time.

explanation.grammar_rule: After quand/lorsque/dĂ¨s que about the future, use the futur simple (not the present).
common_mistakes: using the present after quand (English interference)

tip.pattern: Future time clause â‡’ future tense.
similar: DĂ¨s que jâ€™aurai les chiffres, je tâ€™Ă©crirai.

prompt.fr: Corrigez : Â« Nous prendrons une dĂ©cision quand nous avons toutes les donnĂ©es. Â»

accepted: Nous prendrons une dĂ©cision quand nous aurons toutes les donnĂ©es
Nous prendrons une dĂ©cision quand nous aurons toutes les donnĂ©es.

explanation.correct_why: Future time after quand â†’ future: Â« quand nous aurons toutes les donnĂ©es Â».

distractor_why.present_kept: Â« avons Â» (present) is wrong here; the data is in the future, so use Â« aurons Â».

explanation.grammar_rule: Both clauses are future when quand refers to a future moment.

similar: Je commencerai quand tu seras prĂŞt.

prompt.fr: Associez le verbe Ă  son radical du futur.
prompt.instructions_en: Match each verb to its future stem.

explanation.correct_why: ĂŞtreâ†’ser-, avoirâ†’aur-, allerâ†’ir-, faireâ†’fer-, venirâ†’viendr-.
explanation.grammar_rule: Irregular future stems take the regular endings -ai/-as/-a/-ons/-ez/-ont.
explanation.vocab_notes: These are the most common irregular future stems.
common_mistakes: using the infinitive as the stem for irregular verbs

tip.memory_aid: Same stem for future AND conditional.

prompt.fr: Futur simple : Â« Nous ___ (prendre) une dĂ©cision demain. Â»

explanation.correct_why: -re verbs drop the final -e: prendreâ†’prendr- + -ons = Â« prendrons Â».

distractor_why.kept_e: Drop the -e of -re verbs before the ending.

explanation.grammar_rule: -re verbs: drop final -e, then add future endings.
explanation.vocab_notes: prendre une dĂ©cision = to make a decision.

prompt.fr: Discours indirect : Â« Il dit : â€śJe suis occupĂ©.â€ť Â» â†’ Â« Il dit quâ€™il ___ occupĂ©. Â»
prompt.instructions_en: Reporting with a present introducing verb.

explanation.correct_why: The introducing verb Â« dit Â» is present, so no tense shift: Â« Il dit quâ€™il est occupĂ© Â».

distractor_why.shift_not_needed: Â« Ă©tait Â» (imparfait) is only needed when the introducing verb is in the past.
distractor_why.subjunctive: Â« dire que Â» takes the indicative, not the subjunctive.

explanation.grammar_rule: Present introducing verb â†’ no tense change in reported speech.
common_mistakes: shifting tenses when the introducing verb is present

tip.memory_aid: Present 'dit que' â†’ keep the tense.
similar: Il dit quâ€™il a le dossier.

prompt.fr: Au passĂ© : Â« Il a dit : â€śJe suis occupĂ©.â€ť Â» â†’ Â« Il a dit quâ€™il ___ occupĂ©. Â»
prompt.instructions_en: Past introducing verb â†’ shift the tense.

explanation.correct_why: When the introducing verb is past (Â« a dit Â»), the present shifts to the imparfait: Â« il Ă©tait occupĂ© Â».

distractor_why.present_kept: Â« est Â» doesn't reflect the required past shift after Â« a dit Â».

explanation.grammar_rule: Concordance des temps: present â†’ imparfait after a past reporting verb.
common_mistakes: keeping the present after a past reporting verb

tip.memory_aid: Past report â†’ present becomes imparfait.
similar: Il a dit quâ€™il avait le dossier.

prompt.fr: Question indirecte : Â« Il demande : â€śEst-ce que tu viens?â€ť Â» â†’ Â« Il demande ___ tu viens. Â»

explanation.correct_why: A reported yes/no question uses Â« si Â»: Â« Il demande si tu viens Â».

distractor_why.que_for_statement: Â« que Â» introduces a reported statement, not a yes/no question.
distractor_why.kept_estceque: Â« est-ce que Â» disappears in indirect speech; it becomes Â« si Â».

explanation.grammar_rule: Reported yes/no question: demander si.
common_mistakes: keeping est-ce que in indirect questions

similar: Elle demande si tu es libre.

prompt.fr: Corrigez : Â« Elle a dit quâ€™elle va envoyer le rapport demain. Â» (concordance)
prompt.instructions_en: Apply the tense shift after a past reporting verb.

accepted: Elle a dit quâ€™elle allait envoyer le rapport le lendemain
Elle a dit quâ€™elle enverrait le rapport le lendemain
Elle a dit quâ€™elle allait envoyer le rapport le lendemain.

explanation.correct_why: After Â« a dit Â» (past), the near future Â« va envoyer Â» shifts to Â« allait envoyer Â» (or futureâ†’conditional Â« enverrait Â»), and Â« demain Â» â†’ Â« le lendemain Â».

distractor_why.no_shift: Â« va envoyer Â» keeps the present-time frame; after a past report it must shift.

explanation.grammar_rule: Past reporting: futur proche â†’ imparfait of aller + inf.; futur â†’ conditionnel; demain â†’ le lendemain.
common_mistakes: not shifting tense or time words in past reported speech

tip.memory_aid: Past report shifts both tense and time words.
tip.pattern: a dit que + shifted tense + le lendemain.
similar: Il a dit quâ€™il viendrait le lendemain.
Elle a dit quâ€™elle Ă©tait occupĂ©e ce jour-lĂ .

prompt.fr: Question indirecte : Â« Il demande : â€śQuâ€™est-ce que tu veux?â€ť Â» â†’ Â« Il demande ___ tu veux. Â»

explanation.correct_why: Â« Quâ€™est-ce que Â» becomes Â« ce que Â» in indirect speech: Â« Il demande ce que tu veux Â».

distractor_why.kept: Â« quâ€™est-ce que Â» doesn't survive in indirect speech.
distractor_why.que: Â« que Â» alone introduces a statement, not an embedded 'what' question.

explanation.grammar_rule: Indirect 'what' question: ce que (object) / ce qui (subject).
common_mistakes: keeping est-ce que in indirect questions

similar: Il demande ce qui se passe.
Je ne sais pas ce que tu veux.

prompt.fr: Concordance : Â« Elle a dit : â€śJe viendrai demain.â€ť Â» â†’ Â« Elle a dit quâ€™elle ___ le lendemain. Â»

explanation.correct_why: After Â« a dit Â» (past), the future Â« viendrai Â» becomes the conditional Â« viendrait Â», and Â« demain Â» â†’ Â« le lendemain Â».

distractor_why.future_kept: Â« viendra Â» keeps the future; a past report needs the conditional.

explanation.grammar_rule: Past reporting: future â†’ conditional; demain â†’ le lendemain.

tip.pattern: a dit que + conditional + le lendemain.
similar: Il a dit quâ€™il enverrait le rapport.
